title: School midday and crossing patrol occupations
description: Assist a preschool, elementary, middle, or secondary school teacher with instructional duties. Serve in a position for which a teacher has primary responsibility for the design and implementation of educational programs and services. Assist a preschool, elementary, middle, or secondary school teacher to provide academic, social, or life skills to students who have learning, emotional, or physical disabilities. Serve in a position for which a teacher has primary responsibility for the design and implementation of educational programs and services. All teaching assistants not listed separately. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Tutor and assist children individually or in small groups to help them master assignments and reinforce learning concepts presented by teachers.
Use computers, audio-visual aids, and other equipment and materials to supplement presentations and enhance learning.
Prepare lesson materials, bulletin board displays, and demonstrations for classroom activities.
Present subject matter to students under the direction of teachers using lectures, discussions, and reading aloud.
Requisition and stock teaching materials and supplies to ensure classroom resources are available.
Supervise students in classrooms, halls, cafeterias, school yards, and during field trips for safety and discipline.
Teach social skills and acceptable behavior using techniques like positive reinforcement and behavior modification.
Instruct students in daily living skills needed for independence, such as hygiene, safety, and food preparation.
Organize and supervise games and recreational activities to promote physical and social development among students.
Observe students' performance and record relevant data to assess their progress and provide feedback.
Provide assistance to students with special needs, including access to assistive devices and supportive technology.
Discuss assigned duties with classroom teachers to coordinate instructional efforts and support teaching objectives.
Distribute teaching materials like textbooks and workbooks to students as part of classroom management.
Attend staff meetings and serve on committees as required to collaborate with other educational staff.
Maintain classroom cleanliness and organization to create a suitable learning environment for students.
Participate in teacher-parent conferences regarding students' progress or problems to support educational success.
Assist in the safe loading and unloading of students on school buses as needed.
Monitor compliance with established safety procedures to maintain a secure and supportive school environment.
Examine injured persons and administer first aid or cardiopulmonary resuscitation, if necessary, using training and medical supplies and equipment.
Guide or control vehicular or pedestrian traffic at places such as street and railroad crossings and construction sites.
Direct or escort pedestrians across streets, stopping traffic as necessary, and communicate traffic and crossing rules to students and adults.
Monitor traffic flow to locate safe gaps for pedestrians to cross streets, and report hazards such as missing traffic signals or signs.
Maintain close communications with dispatching personnel, using two-way radios or cell phones, and respond to radio dispatch calls regarding issues such as parking violations.
Write reports documenting activities while on patrol, including observations and any incidents, and maintain files as necessary.
Provide information to the public regarding parking regulations, traffic rules, and safety precautions for recreational activities.
Observe and report unsafe behavior of children to school officials and investigate reports of disruptions or disturbances.
Train new or temporary staff on duties related to traffic management and ensure adherence to safety protocols.
Report unsafe or hazardous driving behaviors to appropriate authorities and issue warnings or citations as necessary.

emerging_tasks.task: Deliver educational presentations on wildlife topics, such as animal safety, at schools and community events.
Operate drones for surveillance of large areas and tracking of wildlife.
Perform traffic control duties such as setting up barricades and temporary signs, placing bags on parking meters to limit their use, or directing traffic or pedestrians.
Pick up deceased animals from roadways and dispose of the animal carcasses.
Testify at court hearings when citations are contested.
Transport injured or ill wildlife to rescues.
Use drone technology for surveillance and loss prevention.
